steps.title: Compute the boundary list
steps.content: All answer choices use $x=3$, $5$, and $7$. Compute the right side of $y\lt6x+2$ for each value: $$6(3)+2=20$$ $$6(5)+2=32$$ $$6(7)+2=44.$$ The $y$-values must be less than $20$, $32$, and $44$, respectively.

steps.title: Compare the candidate to the boundary
steps.content: The correct table has $x$-values $3$, $5$, and $7$ paired with $y$-values $16$, $28$, and $40$. Each output is strictly below the matching cutoff: $16\lt20$, $28\lt32$, and $40\lt44$.

steps.title: Eliminate the failed choices
steps.content: The boundary table with $y$-values $20$, $32$, and $44$ is not strictly below the cutoff. Another table fails because $36$ is above the allowed cutoff $32$, and the remaining table fails immediately at $(3,24)$. The table with all three $y$-values below their cutoffs is correct, so C is correct.
